# pudge-ui

> pudge-ui is an LLM-agnostic, framework-agnostic design system inspired by 2000s
> consumer electronics. It is not a component library — it is a design *specification*
> that any coding agent can consume to generate pixel-perfect, skeuomorphic UI in any
> stack. 93 components across 11 categories, built on 6 material recipes and a shared
> token/depth/material foundation.

The fastest way for an agent to use pudge-ui is the MCP server `@pudge-ui/mcp-server`,
which exposes the specs as tools (`list_components`, `search_components`, `get_foundation`,
`get_component`, `get_components`, `get_composition`). Otherwise, read the foundation
first, then the specific component spec.

## Start here
- [Getting started](https://ui.pudgestudio.com/getting-started): install the MCP server or copy specs manually.
- [For LLMs](https://ui.pudgestudio.com/for-llms): the agent-oriented overview and prompt guidance.

## Foundation (read this first)
- [Philosophy](https://ui.pudgestudio.com/foundation/philosophy)
- [Design tokens](https://ui.pudgestudio.com/foundation/tokens)
- [Materials](https://ui.pudgestudio.com/foundation/materials)
- [Depth model](https://ui.pudgestudio.com/foundation/depth)
- [Naming](https://ui.pudgestudio.com/foundation/naming)

## Components & compositions
- [All components](https://ui.pudgestudio.com/components): browse the 93 component specs by category.
- [Compositions](https://ui.pudgestudio.com/compositions): assembled multi-component layouts (e.g. audio mixer strip).

## Integration
- [Framework integration](https://ui.pudgestudio.com/guides/framework-integration)
- [Native integration](https://ui.pudgestudio.com/guides/native-integration)

## Optional
- [Source & specs on GitHub](https://github.com/pudgestudio/pudge-ui)
- [MCP server on npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@pudge-ui/mcp-server)
